ocr.text: In the rapidly evolving landscape of technology and software, understanding the financial dynamics of leading enterprises is
crucial for stakeholders and investors alike. The accompanying data table provides a comprehensive overview of Salesforce's
performance, with a particular focus on key metrics such as Enterprise License Revenue and SaaS Recurring Revenue.
Metric  Q1Revenue  Q2Revenue  Q3Revenue  Q4Revenue  TotalRevenue  NetIncome  OperatingIncome  CAPEX
RevenueBreakdown  CloudServices  LicenseFees  SupportServices  ProfessionalServices  TotalExpenses  OperatingMargin  NetMargin  EarningsPerShare
These revenue streams are essential indicators of the company’s market position and growth trajectory.
Metric  RevenueBreakdown  GrowthRate(%)
SubscriptionRevenue  CustomerMetrics  ChurnRate(%)  NetPromoterScore
TotalRevenue(£M)  AnnualGrowth(%)  MarketShare(%)  NewCustomers  ExistingCustomers  TotalCustomers  CustomerAcquisitionCost(£)
For instance, the reported Total Revenue of £300M reflects Salesforce's robust business model and commitment to innovation,
particularly in the context of its substantial R&D Expenditure by Division.
Division  R&DExpenditure
SalesforceCloud  SalesforceEinstein
Q1FY2023  Q2FY2023  Q3FY2023  Q4FY2023  TotalFY2023
Marketing  £8.0M  £8.5M  £9.0M  £9.5M
This investment in research and development is critical as it fuels the creation of new products and enhancements to existing
services, thereby attracting both New Customers and retaining Existing Customers. Moreover, the Annual Growth percentage
allows for an assessment of the company’s performance relative to industry benchmarks, highlighting Salesforce's strategic
initiatives in a competitive market. Overall, these metrics not only illustrate Salesforce's current standing but also provide
insights into its future potential within the European technology sector.
